content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, overlapping/dependent features (my global registry needs your local filters to exist so it can defer to them). Propose: you implement local 'filters' param (on prompt()/Prompt, applied to env.filters at template-creation or call time) first since mine builds on it. I'll add module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter(), and merge global filters into env.filters BEFORE your local filters are applied so local ones win. Can you share the exact signature/placement you're planning (e.g. does prompt() gain a filters kwarg, and where do you call env.filters.update)? I'll implement against that and we'll merge via git at the end, resolving any line conflicts manually.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. Registry names: module-level dict outlines.prompts._FILTERS (Dict[str, Callable]), functions register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) and unregister_filter(name: str). If your create_jinja_env() accepts a filters dict, feel free to do env.filters.update(_FILTERS) first then env.filters.update(local_filters) so local overrides global - or I'll wire that merge myself after you push if easier. Starting implementation now.
